text: So here today in New York City with Mike Arnstein, one of the best ultra runners
currently in the US. Fifth fastest American last year in New York City
Marathon, 2.28 marathoner, 32 10k and a winner in some of the 100 mile races here in the US.
So for beginners starting up running Mike, what would you say to
someone who's never ran before, how would you get started in running?
I'd say start walking. You look at other people who've got good fitness and if you
admire that and want what they have, you've got to be willing to do the work.
So number one is you've got to commit to doing the work. You've got to say to yourself
I'm gonna go to sleep at a reasonable hour so I can wake up and feel good and
then you say to yourself I want to feel even better and you get out there and if you
can't run because maybe you're too overweight, you don't look at other
people and say I can't run.
Why should I even bother? Start walking.
Yeah.
And you know you just you just don't give up. You got to have that mentality.
You got to be ready and willing to do the work. If you have that mentality you will
be able to reach any one of your goals at any time whether that's running a
marathon, qualify for Boston or just to finish. You just gotta, it's all up here.
Yeah.
A lot of people look at me and say Mike, you're the best. You're the best.
Yeah.
They look at what I do and they say oh that guy's got talent. He's just lucky.
Well let me tell you, I developed the talent that we all have. We all got natural ability.
The only difference is I've cultivated it more. When I win it's all fair because I
did more work.
Nice.
That's what it's about. Making it happen with no bullshit excuses.
Just do it.
Just get out there and start today hey.
